blocks.content: Back in the house next door, the conversation had moved to the living room. Nobody decided to move it — they just migrated, the way people do when they need a different room for a different kind of talking.

blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: The argument. Last night. Who was he calling?

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: Davenport has his phone. They'll pull the records.

blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: But we heard it. We all heard it. Two voices. His and someone else's.

blocks.speaker: Sophie
blocks.content: It could have been a client. You said yourself he always picked up.

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: On a Saturday night that heated? That wasn't a client call.

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: Because I've known Marcus for twenty-three years and I've heard him take client calls. He gets professional. He goes flat. What we heard through the ceiling was not flat.

blocks.speaker: Wren
blocks.content: It could have been someone from his past. Something we don't know about.

blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: Marcus didn't have enemies. He was the least — out of all of us, he was the one who didn't make enemies.
Wren looked at him for a moment. Then she looked out the window.

blocks.speaker: Wren
blocks.content: We don't always know what people carry.
